steps.title: Find point H on the unit circle
steps.content: Since $H=(-1,y)$ lies on the unit circle $x^{2}+y^{2}=1$: $$(-1)^{2}+y^{2}=1 \Rightarrow y^{2}=0 \Rightarrow y=0.$$ So $H=(-1,0)$, directly opposite $F=(1,0)$.

steps.title: Determine the geometric angle
steps.content: The rays $GF$ and $GH$ point in opposite directions along the x-axis, so the central angle is $\pi$ radians. Any coterminal positive measure has the form $$\pi + 2\pi k = (2k+1)\pi,$$ i.e., an odd multiple of $\pi$.

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
We need an odd positive integer multiple of $\pi$.
  • A) $\frac{27\pi}{2}=13.5\pi$ — not an integer multiple of $\pi$.
  • B) $\frac{29\pi}{2}=14.5\pi$ — not an integer multiple of $\pi$.
  • C) $24\pi$ — even multiple, gives angle $0$, not $\pi$.
  • D) $25\pi = 12(2\pi)+\pi$ — odd multiple, coterminal with $\pi$.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
